title: tensor-defi: Information Geometry of Markets v1.0.0
description: Tensor decomposition framework for cryptocurrency market microstructure. Version 1.0.0 provides proof-of-concept demonstrating that tensor methods preserve multiplicative interactions between asset and temporal dimensions. Using one year of hourly OHLCV data for BTC, ETH, and SOL from Binance, rank-4 CP and Tucker decompositions achieve 96.55% and 96.56% explained variance respectively, outperforming PCA (92.31%) by 4.6 percentage points. Future versions will expand to multi-venue analysis.
references: Kolda, T. G., & Bader, B. W. (2009). Tensor decompositions and applications. SIAM review, 51(3), 455-500.
Lettau, M., & Pelger, M. (2024). Factors That Fit the Time Series and Cross-Section of Stock Returns. The Review of Financial Studies, 37(7), 2193-2228.
Babii, A., Ghysels, E., & Striaukas, J. (2023). Machine learning time series regressions with an application to nowcasting. Journal of Business & Economic Statistics, 41(3), 768-778.
notes: Version 1.0.0: Proof-of-concept on single-venue (Binance) data with 3 assets (BTC, ETH, SOL). Future versions will expand to multi-venue analysis (v2.0), robustness validation (v2.1), higher-dimensional features (v3.0), and information geometry computations (v3.1). Software includes complete reproducibility pipeline: data collection, tensor construction, CP/Tucker decomposition, PCA baselines, and visualization tools.
